steps.title: Identify what both texts scrutinize
steps.content:
Text 1 faults Chen's study for not excluding other nutrient pathways—it lacked the “crucial step” that Alvarez took (using barriers).

Text 2 faults Alvarez's barriers for allowing liquids to pass through, preventing the researchers from isolating CMN.

The shared concern: both texts question whether the studies can actually eliminate non-CMN pathways.

steps.title: Find the question yielding the same answer
steps.content:
B asks whether Chen's study effectively excluded other pathways.

Text 1 explicitly states Chen did not take the crucial barrier step; Text 2, though focused on Alvarez, reveals that even Alvarez's more rigorous design failed to exclude pathways, so Chen's would certainly fail as well.

Both authors would answer No. The other choices either address concerns not shared by both authors (A, D) or would draw opposite answers from the two texts (Text 1 suggests Alvarez's barriers were sufficient; Text 2 says they weren't).

That matches Choice B — Did Chen and colleagues' study effectively exclude any nutrient pathway other than a CMN?.
